seo.title: Building Data Extraction with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build data extraction with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.
keywords: Azure AI Agent Service data extraction

content.intro: Azure AI Agent Service enables robust data extraction from unstructured documents using schema-guided LLM calls with built-in Azure OpenAI integration and enterprise grounding. Vex guardrails are essential to ensure schema compliance, field accuracy, and completeness while mitigating common failures like unvalidated tool results and token limit exceedances. This guide shows how to integrate Vex as middleware for reliable enterprise-grade extraction.

architectureOverview.heading: Data Extraction Architecture with Azure AI Agent Service
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture begins with document ingestion via Azure Blob Storage or File Search, followed by Azure AI Agent Service orchestration. Agents use schema-guided prompts with Azure OpenAI models (GPT-4o) to extract structured data, leveraging built-in grounding from Bing Search and enterprise RAG connectors. Tool calls handle file retrieval and validation, with results flowing through agent threads to produce JSON outputs matching predefined Pydantic schemas.

Processing occurs within agent threads that maintain conversation state, with automatic retries and fallback mechanisms. Outputs are validated against enterprise policies before storage in Cosmos DB or return to calling applications. Vex integrates as middleware in the agent execution pipeline to add verification layers without disrupting native Azure controls.

Key components include the AgentProject client for thread management, schema enforcement via structured outputs, and grounding data sources filtered by enterprise permissions.

risks.name: Tool execution results not validated against enterprise policies
risks.description: Agents execute file search and grounding tools without checking extracted data against compliance rules like PII redaction or format validation
risks.mitigation: Vex policy enforcement middleware validates all tool outputs before agent processing

risks.name: Grounding data from Bing Search containing outdated information
risks.description: Freshness_date validation fails, causing agents to use stale web data for document context
risks.mitigation: Vex temporal validation gates reject grounding data older than configurable threshold

risks.name: File search returning Azure-specific metadata instead of content
risks.description: RAG tools return blob metadata/properties instead of document text content
risks.mitigation: Vex content extraction guard ensures only relevant document text reaches LLM

risks.name: Agent thread token limits exceeded without graceful degradation
risks.description: Long documents cause thread token overflow, leading to hallucinated or truncated extractions
risks.mitigation: Vex token budget monitoring with automatic summarization fallbacks

implementation.title: Basic Schema-Guided Extraction Agent
implementation.description: Create agent project with Pydantic schema for invoice extraction using Azure OpenAI
implementation.code:
from azure.ai.projects import AgentProject
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

class InvoiceSchema(BaseModel):
    invoice_number: str = Field(..., description="Invoice ID")
    total_amount: float = Field(..., description="Total USD amount")
    date: str = Field(..., description="Invoice date YYYY-MM-DD")

project = AgentProject.from_config("azure_config.json")
agent = project.agents.create(
    name="invoice_extractor",
    instructions="Extract invoice data using the provided schema",
    model="gpt-4o",
    output_type=InvoiceSchema
)

thread = project.threads.create()
result = agent.run(
    thread,
    "Extract from: " + document_text
)

print(result.output)

implementation.title: File Search Tool Integration
implementation.description: Configure file search tool with enterprise connectors for document retrieval
implementation.code:
from azure.ai.projects import FileSearchTool

file_search = FileSearchTool(
    name="enterprise_docs",
    connection_id="doc-indexer-connection",
    query_type="semantic",
    fields_mapping={"content": "content_field"}
)

agent = project.agents.create(
    name="doc_extractor",
    tools=[file_search],
    instructions="Search docs then extract using schema"
)

vexIntegration.title: Vex Middleware Pipeline Setup
vexIntegration.description: Wrap Azure agent execution with comprehensive Vex verification layers
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import Pipeline, Guardrail
from azure.ai.projects import AgentProject

pipeline = Pipeline([
    Guardrail.schema_compliance(),
    Guardrail.field_accuracy(min_confidence=0.9),
    Guardrail.completeness_check(),
    Guardrail.type_consistency(),
    Guardrail.token_budget(max_tokens=128000)
])

@ vex.middleware
def guarded_agent_run(agent: AgentProject.Agent, input_text: str):
    thread = agent.project.threads.create()
    raw_result = agent.run(thread, input_text)
    verified = pipeline.run(raw_result)
    return verified

vexIntegration.title: Policy Enforcement for Tool Results
vexIntegration.description: Validate file search and grounding outputs before LLM processing
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import PolicyGuard

policy_guard = PolicyGuard([
    "no_pii_exposure",
    "content_type:document",
    "freshness:24h",
    "azure_metadata_filtered"
])

def safe_tool_call(tool_name: str, params: dict):
    raw_result = azure_agent.call_tool(tool_name, params)
    verified_result = policy_guard.enforce(raw_result)
    return verified_result.content
